---
title: "Projects"
subtitle: "The work I spend the most time on. Each links to a longer write-up."
toc: false
---

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
[West Nile virus thesis — California mosquito surveillance](projects/wnv-thesis.qmd){.card-title}

A quantitative MPH thesis modeling WNV infection rate in *Culex* mosquito pools
against environmental covariates and surveillance effort, with spatial and
temporal structure handled explicitly on a county-month grid. In progress; no
results claimed yet.

[Spatial epidemiology]{.tag} [Bayesian modeling]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[MESA — Mapping Epidemiology in Sparse Areas](projects/mesa.qmd){.card-title}

A C++ desktop application for public-health mapping where data is thin:
geocoding, Getis-Ord Gi\* and Moran's I, and Monte Carlo permutation testing,
with the statistics validated against PySAL and R.

[Spatial statistics]{.tag} [C++]{.tag} [Desktop software]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[FEMA Hazus — GIS and risk-modeling software](projects/hazus.qmd){.card-title}

Software in support of FEMA's Hazus natural-hazard loss-estimation program:
GIS-driven hazard and risk workflows, spatial data engineering, database
updates, and validation. Kept high-level and sanitized.

[GIS]{.tag} [Risk modeling]{.tag} [C#/C++]{.tag} [SQL Server]{.tag}
:::

::: {.project-card}
[SEUHD practicum — applied public-health GIS](projects/seuhd-practicum.qmd){.card-title}

Practicum with a local health department: respiratory-illness spatial analysis
with space-time cubes and emerging hot-spot analysis, plus immunization-
compliance reporting from EHR data and geocoded addresses.

[Applied epidemiology]{.tag} [ArcGIS]{.tag} [Surveillance data]{.tag} [Public health]{.tag}
:::

:::

## Code & open source

A few public repositories relevant to this work — disease modeling, biostatistics,
and environmental data. The full list, including GIS and other tooling, is at
[github.com/minorsecond](https://github.com/minorsecond).

::: {.repo-list}

::: {.repo-item}
[Host–vector–human transmission model](https://github.com/minorsecond/host-vector-human-model){.repo-name}

A spatial agent-based simulation of arbovirus (Zika) transmission: human and
mosquito agents are placed within census tracts and the model steps daily
through vector, sexual, perinatal, and transfusion transmission routes, backed
by a PostGIS database. A research prototype rather than a finished tool.

[Spatial epidemiology]{.tag} [Agent-based model]{.tag} [Vector-borne disease]{.tag} [PostGIS]{.tag} [Python]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[Stochastic SEIR epidemic model](https://github.com/minorsecond/Epi){.repo-name}

An individual-based stochastic SEIR model: each person moves through
susceptible–exposed–infectious–removed states with Gaussian-drawn incubation
and infectious periods, optional waning immunity, and mortality. Computes R₀ and
a vaccination threshold and writes the epidemic curve to CSV.

[Infectious disease]{.tag} [SEIR]{.tag} [Stochastic simulation]{.tag} [Perl]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[Behavioral risk factors for stroke (BRFSS)](https://github.com/minorsecond/bios5300){.repo-name}

A graduate biostatistics study of stroke and behavioral/mental-health factors
(drinking, smoking, anxiety, depression, sleep) in 2016 BRFSS data, using
survey-weighted quasi-binomial logistic regression with correct stratification
and clustering, odds ratios with confidence intervals, and a state-level
prevalence map. Written up as a reproducible RMarkdown report.

[Biostatistics]{.tag} [Complex survey design]{.tag} [Logistic regression]{.tag} [BRFSS]{.tag} [R]{.tag}
:::

::: {.repo-item}
[Colorado forest-fragmentation web map](https://github.com/minorsecond/co_forestfrag_map){.repo-name}

An interactive OpenLayers web map of Colorado land cover and forest-fragmentation
change (2001–2016), with a time slider over WMS raster layers and county- and
hex-grid aggregations served from a GeoServer backend. Environmental data
communication rather than a health analysis.

[Web GIS]{.tag} [OpenLayers]{.tag} [Environmental data]{.tag} [Raster time series]{.tag}
:::

:::
